title: eleocharis quinqueflora subsp. fernaldii
intro: Eleocharis quinqueflora subsp. fernaldii is a subspecies of sedge, also known as a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the genus Eleocharis. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots and stems being used to create herbal remedies. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in wound healing and immune system function. Eleocharis quinqueflora subsp. fernaldii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in tinctures or salves for topical application.
